behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names schema path, test file path, specific hex values (7ff8000000000001, 7ff0000000000000, fff0000000000000), signature UUID, pattern, and required fields.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the broken behavior and what the schema/test must contain, not exact code diffs.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check schema structure and runtime test behavior, not specific function names or line positions; alternative schemas satisfying the oneOf entry would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run go test subprocesses and validate JSON samples with jsonschema.validate.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert the flaky go test passes reliably and validate actual float samples against the schema; stubs wouldn't satisfy this.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Schema structure, generator integration (via go test execution), and pass-to-pass checks are all covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings distinguishing f2p vs p2p.

structured_data_schema.reason: JSON schema fields (signature key, const UUID, pattern) are explicitly documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers appear consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file being fixed but localization is part of the verification criteria, not a debugging hint.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code/schema task, not an agentmd-edits config task.
